== name in kana (P1814) == * おづじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 524-0062 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7160005008941 == Commons category (P373) == * Ozu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市杉江町495', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ozu-jinja}} [[JA:小津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97206855}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おづじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 524-0062 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7160005008941 == Commons category (P373) == * Ozu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市杉江町495', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ozu-jinja}} [[JA:小津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97206855}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{'text': '滋賀県守山市杉江町495', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ozu-jinja}} [[JA:小津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97206855}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{'text': '滋賀県守山市杉江町495', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ozu-jinja}} [[JA:小津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97206855}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{'text': '滋賀県守山市杉江町495', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ozu-jinja}} [[JA:小津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97206855}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

{'text': '滋賀県守山市杉江町495', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ozu-jinja}} [[JA:小津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97206855}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

{'text': '滋賀県守山市杉江町495', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ozu-jinja}} [[JA:小津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97206855}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}|shrine in {{ill|lt=Kōchi|Q200251|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}|Ozu Shrine (Kōchi)}} {{神社 | 名称=Ozu Shrine | 画像=[[File:小津神社-拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}495 Sugie-chō | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|4|22.875|N|135|57|36.043|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} | 神体= | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ozu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ozu Shrine''' (おづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Precinct === It stands in a forest 4.3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/168|title=Ozu Shrine | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. The shrine lies at the center of Sugie, a settlement that was a key point for transport on Lake Biwa waterways{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. The precinct area is 28,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|1=Square metre}}, with the honden standing amid old trees. === History === The shrine originally worshiped the ancient local Ozu clan; later {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} became the principal deity. Shrine records say that in {{ill|lt=567|Q32555|WD=Q32555|1=567}} the shrine buildings were washed out into {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} by flood{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450|title=Shrine introduction: Ozu Shrine (OZU)|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. The dance dedicated by parishioners when welcoming back the divine spirit that had flowed into the lake is said to have become the later Naginata festival{{Cite book|和書|title=Moriyama City Bulletin|date=2000-05-01|year=2000|publisher=Moriyama City|url=http://www2.city.moriyama.lg.jp/koho/000501/index3.html|accessdate=2020-04-15|page=3}}. In {{ill|lt=Bun'ō|Q1199883|WD=Q1199883|1=Bun'ō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1260|Q5508|WD=Q5508|1=1260}}), offerings from Sagami-no-kami and Echigo-no-kami and donations of Azumigawa fishing grounds were made. Revered by the imperial court and warrior houses, in {{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}} ({{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 2) Ashikaga Takauji prohibited armed troops from entering the sacred precinct. The honden burned in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin War|Q385676|WD=Q385676|1=Ōnin War}}, then was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Rokkaku Takayori|Q2890348|WD=Q2890348|1=Rokkaku Takayori}}. It burned again in {{ill|lt=1505|Q6153|WD=Q6153|1=1505}} ({{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} 2) during the Eishō disturbances, but the current honden was rebuilt over 18 years by the Enryakuji monk Myōshunbō Jikkan. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it had 13 nearby villages as ujiko{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deity is Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto; later {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kamiōichi-hime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Ōichi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} were enshrined as associated deities. === Festival === Naginata dance is performed every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/event/detail/118|title=Naginata-furi (Ozu Shrine) | Shiga tourism information|accessdate=2020-04-15|publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau}}. With mikoshi procession, ritual performances are dedicated by rotating duty groups of carriers and dancers{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. In years when Sugie, Akanoi, Yajima, Miyake, Yamaga, and Ishida serve as dance groups, offerings include naginata-furi and Sanyare dance{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Naginata-furi leads the parade while swinging naginata; Sanyare dancers follow in flower-hat costume with {{ill|lt=taiko|Q11404|WD=Q11404|1=Drum}}, {{ill|lt=kane|Q3482784|WD=Q3482784|1=Shōko (instrument)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sasara|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=flute|Q11405|WD=Q11405|1=Flute}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. Three large mikoshi and two children's mikoshi travel round-trip to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ozu Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Akanoi about 1 km away. Such dances are called {{ill|lt=fūryū odori|Q11665528|WD=Q11665528|1=Furyū-odori}} and are said to have spread from Kyoto across Shiga in the early modern period{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Statue of Ukanomitama at Ozu-jinja.jpg|thumb|Wooden seated Ukanomitama statue (Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden seated statue of Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto : The goddess statue enshrined in the honden is estimated to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} and is designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=184}}. It is a painted wooden image about 50 cm high with a lotus crown, left hand holding a jewel on a raised left knee. It is counted among three deity statues together with examples at {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|WD=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-ji|Q945913|WD=Q945913|1=Yakushi-ji}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Ōmi Kenketo festival naginata-furi === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historical heritage of Shiga along old roads|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Section}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture|Q11564591|WD=Q11564591|1=List of shrines in Shiga Prefecture}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ozu-jinja}} * [https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=450 Ozu Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-350/ Ozu Shrine] - Moriyama Tourism & Products Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ozu Shrine (Moriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]
